title: Selenicereus extensus
taxonomy: Selenicereus extensus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Cactaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Selenicereus, which is a group of epiphytic cacti native to the Americas. Selenicereus extensus is further categorized as a type of climbing cactus, characterized by its long, thin stems and trumpet-shaped flowers. The plant's classification is also influenced by its habit of growing on other plants or trees, which is a common trait among epiphytic cacti. As a member of the Cactaceae family, Selenicereus extensus shares characteristics with other cacti, such as thick, water-storing stems and spines for protection.
uses: Selenicereus extensus, also known as the Claret Cup Cactus, has been used in traditional medicine for its purported health benefits. The plant's extracts have been studied for their potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. In some cultures, it is used to treat various ailments, including fever and respiratory issues. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption. The plant's medicinal uses are largely based on its traditional applications, and its potential as a therapeutic agent is still being explored.

intro: Selenicereus extensus, also known as the Queen of the Night or Night-blooming Cereus, is a species of cactus native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, characterized by its white or pale pink flowers that bloom in the evening. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation, pain, and fever, due to its reported health benefits such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Selenicereus extensus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, including digestive issues and skin problems.
